key_reasoning: The scenario requires a compound condition: a meaningfully disruptive cyber attack on German critical infrastructure AND German government public attribution within 30 days. Wack's predetermined-vs-uncertain taxonomy identifies two structural constraints that bind simultaneously. First, Germany's non-belligerent status is a predetermined element — Germany declined coalition participation in this conflict, preserving Iran's European diplomatic channel; Iran's strategic interest in that channel is load-bearing for the Pakistan/Qatar mediation architecture, which Iran actively engages. Attacking a NATO member's critical infrastructure destroys that channel at maximum cost. Second, German public attribution within 30 days is structurally near-impossible: German institutional attribution processes historically run 6-12 months, and the political will to publicly accuse Iran while other EU governments avoid the primary conflict is a predetermined institutional friction. The only upward force is PWL-026 (IRGC 'Dindoor' cyber escalation, Ansoff Level 3): IRGC cyber command is actively developing new capabilities under wartime conditions, but the documented target set is US networks, not European infrastructure. IRGC institutional autonomy creates residual tail risk of unauthorised escalation beyond authorised targeting doctrine, but the compound probability (meaningful disruption AND public attribution within 30 days) lands below the prior.

predetermined_elements_bearing_on_scenar: IRGC cyber capability confirmed expanding under wartime conditions (MuddyWater 'Dindoor' backdoor, PWL-026, Ansoff Level 3)
IRGC documented 2026 target set is US healthcare and financial networks — no European critical infrastructure targeting signal in intelligence base
Germany declined military coalition membership in current conflict — not a primary belligerent (PWL-006 retirement, cycle 260316-1230)
Germany is a NATO member — critical infrastructure attack invokes collective defense calculus that IRGC institutional autonomy does not override at strategic level
Iran's strategic interest in European diplomatic neutrality is structurally active — Pakistan/Qatar/Turkey mediation channels depend on European legitimacy cover that Iran has not discarded
German government public attribution processes are structurally slow (institutional predetermined) — 6-12 months is the historical baseline for state-level cyber attribution in Germany
genuine_uncertainties: Whether IRGC has pre-positioned access to German energy/water/rail infrastructure (no signal in peripheral watch list or intelligence base)
Whether IRGC authorisation exists for NATO European critical infrastructure operations (autonomy is real but European targeting implies collective defense exposure IRGC leadership would weigh)
Whether Germany holds prior intelligence that would compress its attribution timeline below historical baseline
framework_specific.binding_constraint_classification: Attribution requirement is the binding Wack constraint. P(meaningful disruption by Iran/proxy within 30 days) is estimated at 6-8%, constrained by absence of German-specific signals and Iran's strategic interest in European neutrality. P(public German attribution within 30 days | disruption occurred) is estimated at 12-18%, constrained by institutional processes. Compound: approximately 0.8-1.4%. Point estimate 1.3% rounds upward to reflect IRGC institutional autonomy tail (unauthorized European escalation is a low-probability but real residual).
framework_specific.prior_comparison: Prior 0.022 → posterior 0.013. Modest downward revision: no German-specific targeting signal exists in 45+ days of intelligence base; attribution timeline is a structural constraint, not an empirical uncertainty; Germany's non-belligerent status is predetermined. Prior was already appropriately conservative; Wack framework identifies two compounding structural constraints the base rate may not fully capture.
pwl_references: PWL-026: IRGC MuddyWater 'Dindoor' backdoor / cyber escalation — ACTIVE, Ansoff Level 3, created 260327-1115, no updates since; targets US networks
cycle_predetermined_elements_relevant: IRGC institutional autonomy exceeds civilian control (cycle 260510-1800, confirmed)
Institutional consensus against compromise spans civilian and military leadership — conflict expanding, not de-escalating
Iran has strategic incentive to preserve European diplomatic channels while maximising US pressure
